narration: Seventy percent of lottery winners are broke within five years of their win. Wild, right? Here's the psychology no one tells you about.
visual: Dark background. Lottery ticket SVG springs in from below. '70%' counter animates up in giant amber text. Amber bars sweep across top and bottom. Text: GO BROKE IN 5 YEARS.

narration: Suddenly getting millions rewires how your brain calculates 'normal.' A coffee feels cheap. A four-million-dollar mansion? Totally reasonable. It's called hedonic recalibration.
visual: Light background. Coffee cup SVG springs in from left, mansion SVG from right. Price tags animate in: '$6 — CHEAP?' and '$4M — REASONABLE?' Text: BRAIN RECALIBRATES WHAT'S NORMAL.

narration: In year one, winners average over four hundred requests for money — from friends, family, even strangers. Most can't say no, and give away tens of thousands.
visual: Dark background. Center person SVG surrounded by 8 smaller silhouettes with '$$$' labels reaching in. Counter animates 0 to 400+. Text: REQUESTS IN YEAR ONE.

narration: Then the IRS shows up. A fifty-million jackpot becomes thirty million as a lump sum, then drops to around seventeen million after taxes. Less than half.
visual: Light background. Three animated bars growing in sequence: JACKPOT $50M (amber), LUMP SUM $30M (orange), AFTER TAX ~$17M (red). Title: WHAT YOU ACTUALLY KEEP.

narration: The cruelest part? Happiness returns to your baseline in about twelve months. The mansion feels ordinary, excitement fades — but the spending never stops.
visual: Dark background. SVG line graph with happiness curve peaking at WIN marker then falling back to baseline. Amber dot traces the curve. Month labels M3 M6 M9 M12. Title: HAPPINESS RESETS IN 12 MONTHS.

narration: This hits any windfall — bonus, inheritance, same trap. Wait six months, invest eighty percent, tell no one. Follow for more money psychology.
visual: Light background. Piggy bank SVG with coins dropping in. Three rules reveal one by one: 01 WAIT 6 MONTHS, 02 INVEST 80%, 03 TELL NO ONE. Amber FOLLOW FOR MORE button fades in.
